title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Automation and Data Client Systems · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production data release board evaluating a changed source contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for request construction, method semantics, safety, and idempotency, producing method-aware request builder. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers method-aware request builder. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Distinguish safe and idempotent method semantics from what one particular server happens to do. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use GET and HEAD with conditional, range, cache, and body expectations that preserve their defined semantics. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Preconditions Concurrency
steps.instruction:
quiz case — production data release candidate must repeat request P-6 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing. Competency probe: Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Preconditions Concurrency.
